# @vllnt/convex-consent

> Append-only consent ledger (GDPR Art. 6/7) ‚Äî record, withdraw, and gate consent per subject + purpose as a Convex component

`@vllnt/convex-consent` is a publishable Convex component. It targets `convex@^1.41.0`.
It stores two sandboxed tables. `consentEvents` is the append-only legal ledger (one immutable row per grant/deny/withdraw, never mutated ‚Äî the GDPR proof ‚Äî holding an opaque host `subjectRef`, a `purpose` key, a `decision` of `granted`/`denied`/`withdrawn`, the policy `version`, opaque host `proof`, and a server `at` timestamp). `consentState` is the derived current-decision projection ‚Äî exactly one row per `(subjectRef, purpose)` ‚Äî the O(1) runtime gate. A host mutation calls `record(subjectRef, purpose, decision, { version?, proof? })` (appends an event + updates the projection) or `withdraw(subjectRef, purpose, proof?)` (GDPR Art. 7(3) ‚Äî appends a `withdrawn` event, but only when a grant is currently held, else throws `NO_CONSENT`/`NOT_GRANTED`). Before processing personal data the host calls `check(subjectRef, purpose, requiredVersion?)` ‚Äî the gate ‚Äî which returns `granted`/`stale` so a grant for an older policy `version` re-prompts; `getState`, `getStatesForSubject`, and paginated `history` read the record. Time is read from the server clock inside every handler ‚Äî no caller-supplied clock. A bounded, self-rescheduling `prune` (and a built-in daily cron) sweeps superseded ledger events past a retention window; the current-state projection is never pruned. The host owns the subject, the purpose meaning, auth, and the `proof` type (the client is generic over it, with an optional `proofValidator` parser narrowing the opaque stored value at the boundary ‚Äî never a `v.any()` dump); the component owns only the consent record. Mount-safe: it runs correctly under multiple named `app.use` mounts.

## Examples

- [Example component wrapper](example/convex/example.ts): Host-app wrapper functions
- [Example tests](example/convex/example.test.ts): End-to-end usage coverage with `convex-test`
